dtcs.description: Theft Deterrent System Communication Line High Fixation If the communication line (EFIO-IMI) to the transponder key ECU is stuck on HI output, the ECM outputs this DTC. HINT * If the transponder key ECU assembly is replaced, register the key and ECU communication ID. * If the ECM is replaced, register the ECU communication ID. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Check for DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT If any DTCs other than B279A are output, troubleshoot those DTCs first. B --  GO TO DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE CHART  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Engine Immobiliser System A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (TRANSPONDER KEY ECU - ECM) (a) Disconnect the T10 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the E7 or E14 ECM connector. (c) Measure the resistance and voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: *1: for 1GR-FE *2: for 2TR-FE Standard Voltage: (d) Reconnect the ECU and ECM connector. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT TRANSPONDER KEY ECU (OUTPUT) (a) Measure the waveform. Waveform 1 OK: Waveform is output normally (see illustration). Result C --  REPLACE ECM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal B --  REPLACE ECM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TRANSPONDER KEY ECU (POWER SOURCE AND BODY GROUND) (a) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm System Transmitter/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: is not output. NG -- REPLACE ECM See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

steps.instruction: CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
CHECK FOR DTC (a) Check for DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

dtcs.description: 31 - Center Airbag Sensor Assembly Malfunction The center airbag sensor assembly consists of parts such as the airbag sensor, the safing sensor, the drive circuit, the diagnostic circuit and the ignition control. When the center airbag sensor assembly receives signals from the airbag sensor, it determines whether or not the SRS should be activated.

steps.instruction: CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (e) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (g) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (h) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK:

dtcs.description: 13 and B1615/14 are not output. HINT DTCs other than B1610/13 and B1615/14 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG -- REPLACE FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR RH AND LH OK -- END Part 1

steps.instruction: CHECK ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE (FOR OPEN) (a) Disconnect the engine room main wire connector from the instrument panel wire. HINT The service wire has already been inserted into connector E. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE

CHECK ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE (FOR SHORT) (a) Disconnect the engine room main wire connector from the instrument panel wire. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE

CHECK ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE (TO B+) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the engine room main wire connector from the instrument panel wire. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (f) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE

CHECK ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE (TO GROUND) (a) Disconnect the engine room main wire connector from the instrument panel wire. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor assembly and the front airbag sensor LH. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Check that the connectors (on the center airbag sensor assembly side and front airbag sensor LH side) are not damaged    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE WIRE HARNESS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR LH CIRCUIT (FOR OPEN) (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor assembly and the front airbag sensor LH. (b) Using a service wire, connect A11-2 (+SL) and A11-1 (-SL) of connector E. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert the service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE (FOR OPEN) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR LH CIRCUIT (FOR SHORT) (a) Disconnect the service wire from connector E. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE (FOR SHORT) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR LH CIRCUIT (TO B+) (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (c) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: NG --  CHECK ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE (TO B+) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR LH CIRCUIT (TO GROUND) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE (TO GROUND) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR LH (a) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (b) Interchange the front airbag sensor RH with the front airbag sensor LH and connect the connectors to them. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (e) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (g) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (h) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. Result: C --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK B --  REPLACE FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR LH A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the center airbag sensor assembly    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle when possible. (d) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (i) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (j) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK:

dtcs.description: 14 - Front Airbag Sensor LH Circuit Malfunction The front airbag sensor RH consists of parts such as the diagnostic circuit and the frontal detection sensor. The configuration of the front airbag sensor LH is the same as that of the RH. When the center airbag sensor assembly receives signals from the frontal deceleration sensor, it determines whether or not the SRS should be activated.

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Proceed to the appropriate step according to DTC readings. (1) Check the DTC    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. Result: D --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK C --  CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS B --  CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor assembly and the airbag sensor front RH. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE WIRE HARNESS OK -- Continue to next step.

(b) Using a service wire, connect A12-2 (+SR) and A12-1 (-SR) of connector E. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert the service wires into the terminals of the connector when connecting. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE (FOR OPEN) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR RH CIRCUIT (FOR SHORT) (a) Disconnect the service wire from connector E. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE (FOR SHORT) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR RH CIRCUIT (TO B+) (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (c) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: NG --  CHECK ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE (TO B+) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR RH CIRCUIT (TO GROUND) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE (TO GROUND) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR RH (a) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (b) Interchange the airbag sensor front RH with the front airbag sensor LH and connect the connectors to them. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (e) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (g) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (h) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. Result: C --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK B --  REPLACE FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR RH A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the center airbag sensor assembly    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle when possible. (d) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (i) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (j) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK:

steps.instruction: REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the center airbag sensor assembly    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle when possible. (d) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (i) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (j) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK:

OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE WIRE HARNESS OK -- Continue to next step.

(b) Using SST, connect S12-4 (BBR+) and S12-3 (BBR-) of connector C. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

(c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor assembly and the side airbag sensor assembly RH. OK: The connectors are properly connected. CHECK FLOOR WIRE (TO GROUND) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step. Part 2

CHECK SIDE AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY RH (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Connect the connectors to the rear airbag sensor LH and the rear airbag sensor RH. (d) Replace the center airbag sensor assembly    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle when possible. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (i) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (j) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK:

dtcs.description: 22 is set when a malfunction is detected in the side airbag sensor assembly (front passenger seat side) circuit. NOTICE: In order to prevent unexpected airbag deployment, disconnect the following connectors before inspecting parts such as wire harnesses, if the application of tester probes to the center airbag sensor assembly connector is necessary. * (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. * (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. * (c) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor assembly. * (d) Disconnect the connectors from the steering pad. * (e) Disconnect the connector from the front passenger airbag assembly. * (f) Disconnect the connector from the front seat airbag assembly LH. * (g) Disconnect the connector from the front seat airbag assembly RH. HINT Skip the following steps if side and curtain shield airbags are not fitted. * (h) Disconnect the connector from the curtain shield airbag assembly LH. * (i) Disconnect the connector from the curtain shield airbag assembly RH. * (j) Disconnect the connector from the front seat outer belt assembly LH. * (k) Disconnect the connector from the front seat outer belt assembly RH. PROCEDURE

(d) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (i) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. Result: C --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK B --  REPLACE REAR AIRBAG SENSOR RH A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SIDE AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY RH (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Return the rear airbag sensor LH and RH to their original positions and connect the connectors to them. (d) Interchange the side airbag sensor assembly RH with the side airbag sensor assembly LH and connect the connectors to them. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (b) Replace the rear airbag sensor RH and the rear airbag sensor LH for Double Cab    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Impact Sensor/Service and Repair/Rear Airbag Sensor (for Double Cab)/Removal or for Access Cab    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Impact Sensor/Service and Repair/Rear Airbag Sensor (for Access Cab)/Removal. (e) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. dtcs.description: 24 - Front Passenger Side Rear Airbag Sensor Circuit Malfunction The airbag sensor LH consists of parts such as the safing sensor, the diagnostic circuit and the lateral deceleration sensor. The configuration of the rear airbag sensor RH is the same as that of the LH. When the center airbag sensor assembly receives signals from the lateral deceleration sensor, it determines whether or not the SRS should be activated.

(b) Interchange the rear airbag sensor LH with the rear airbag sensor RH and connect the connectors to them. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (e) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (g) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (h) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. Result: C --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK B --  REPLACE REAR AIRBAG SENSOR LH A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SIDE AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY LH (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Return the rear airbag sensor LH and RH to their original positions and connect the connectors to them. (d) Interchange the side airbag sensor assembly LH with the side airbag sensor assembly RH and connect the connectors to them. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (i) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (j) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. Result: HINT DTCs other than B1630/23 and B1635/24 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. C --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK B --  REPLACE SIDE AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY LH A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the center airbag sensor assembly    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle when possible. (d) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (i) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (j) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK:

REPLACE REAR AIRBAG SENSOR RH (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the rear airbag sensor RH for Double Cab    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Impact Sensor/Service and Repair/Rear Airbag Sensor (for Double Cab)/Removal or for Access Cab    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Impact Sensor/Service and Repair/Rear Airbag Sensor (for Access Cab)/Removal. (d) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (i) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (j) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK:

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the side airbag sensor assembly LH and the rear airbag sensor LH. (d) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the side airbag sensor assembly RH and the rear airbag sensor RH. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Check that the connectors (on the side airbag sensor assembly LH side and airbag sensor rear LH side) are not damaged    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure. (b) Check that the connectors (on the side airbag sensor assembly RH side and airbag sensor rear RH side) are not damaged    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE WIRE HARNESS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE (FOR OPEN) SST : 09843-18040 (a) Disconnect the connectors from the side airbag sensor assembly LH and the rear airbag sensor LH. (b) Using SST, connect S13-2 (BCL+) and S13-1 (BCL-) of connector C. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE (FOR SHORT) (a) Disconnect the SST from connector C. (b) Disconnect the connectors from the side airbag sensor assembly LH and rear airbag sensor LH. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE (TO B+) (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (c) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE (TO GROUND) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FLOOR WIRE (FOR OPEN) SST : 09843-18040 (a) Disconnect the connectors from the side airbag sensor assembly RH and the rear airbag sensor RH. (b) Using SST, connect S14-2 (BCR+) and S14-1 (BCR-) of connector C. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.
CHECK FLOOR WIRE (FOR SHORT) (a) Disconnect the SST from connector C. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FLOOR WIRE (TO B+) (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (c) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FLOOR WIRE (TO GROUND) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the center airbag sensor assembly    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle when possible. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (f) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (g) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (i) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK:

dtcs.description: 32 - Occupant Classification System Malfunction The occupant classification system circuit consists of the center airbag sensor assembly and the occupant classification system. When the center airbag sensor assembly receives signals from the occupant classification ECU, it determines whether or not the front passenger airbag assembly, front seat airbag assembly RH and seat belt pretensioner RH should be operated.

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (b) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (d) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (e) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK:

CHECK DTC (OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION ECU) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 10 seconds. (b) Using the Techstream, check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type)/Diagnosis System. OK: DTC is not output. NG --  GO TO DTC CHART OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor assembly and the occupant classification ECU. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Check that the connectors (on the center airbag sensor assembly side and occupant classification ECU side) are not damaged    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE WIRE HARNESS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION SYSTEM CIRCUIT (FOR OPEN) (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor assembly and the occupant classification ECU. (b) Separate Seat : Using a service wire, connect O5-8 (FSR+) and O5-4 (FSR-) of connector E. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert a service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting. (c) Bench Seat : Using a service wire, connect O10-11 (FSR+) and O10-12 (FSR-) of connector E. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert a service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting. (d) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (FOR OPEN) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION SYSTEM CIRCUIT (FOR SHORT) (a) Disconnect the service wire from connector E. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (FOR SHORT) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION SYSTEM CIRCUIT (TO B+) (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (c) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: NG --  CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (TO B+) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION SYSTEM CIRCUIT (TO GROUND) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (TO GROUND) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the center airbag sensor assembly    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle when possible. (d) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (i) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (j) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. Result: HINT DTCs other than B1650/32 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. C --  END B --  REPLACE OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION ECU A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION ECU (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the occupant classification ECU    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Occupant Classification Module - Air Bag/Service and Repair/Occupant Classification ECU (for Separate Seat Type)/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (d) Using the Techstream, perform the zero point calibration    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type). OK: COMPLETED is displayed on the tester. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM SENSITIVITY CHECK (a) Using the Techstream, perform the sensitivity check    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type). Standard Value: 27 to 33 kg (59.52 to 72.75 lb) NEXT --  END

CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (FOR OPEN) (a) Disconnect the No. 1 seat wire connector from the instrument panel wire. HINT The service wire has already been inserted into connector E. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: *1 : Separate Seat *2 : Bench Seat NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE

CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (FOR SHORT) (a) Disconnect the No. 1 seat wire connector from the instrument panel wire. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: *1 : Separate Seat *2 : Bench Seat NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE

CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (TO B+) (a) Disconnect the No. 1 seat wire connector from the instrument panel wire. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (d) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: *1 : Separate Seat *2 : Bench Seat NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE

CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (TO GROUND) (a) Disconnect the No. 1 seat wire connector from the instrument panel wire. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: *1 : Separate Seat *2 : Bench Seat NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE

REPLACE OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION ECU (a) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (b) Using the Techstream, read the current setting data stored in the occupant classification ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type)/Registration. HINT If the previous ECU data cannot be stored in the Techstream, replace the front seat cushion assembly (with occupant classification ECU, occupant classification sensor). (c) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (d) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (e) Replace the occupant classification ECU    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Occupant Classification Module - Air Bag/Service and Repair/Occupant Classification ECU (for Bench Seat Type)/Removal. (f) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery. (g) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (h) Using the Techstream, write the setting data read from the previously installed occupant classification ECU into the newly installed occupant classification ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type)/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM SENSITIVITY CHECK (a) Perform the sensitivity check    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type)/Initialization. NEXT --  END

dtcs.description: 33 is not output. HINT DTCs other than B1651/33 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG -- REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY OK -- USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK

(c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (FOR SHORT) (a) Disconnect the service wire from connector C. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (TO B+) (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (c) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (TO GROUND) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK:

dtcs.description: 35 is not output. HINT DTCs other than B1653/35 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG -- REPLACE SEAT POSITION SENSOR OK -- USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System

steps.instruction: CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. C --  REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR B --  CHECK SEAT POSITION SENSOR CIRCUIT (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - SEAT POSITION SENSOR) A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - SEAT POSITION SENSOR) (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: (d) Turn the ignition switch off. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert the service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting the wire. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. steps.instruction: CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor assembly and the seat position sensor. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Disconnect the connector from the center airbag sensor assembly. (b) Disconnect the connector from the front seat inner belt assembly LH. dtcs.description: 43 - Passenger Airbag ON/OFF Indicator Circuit Malfunction The passenger airbag ON/OFF indicator light circuit consists of the center airbag sensor assembly and the passenger seat belt warning light assembly. This circuit indicates the operation condition of the front passenger airbag assembly, the front seat airbag assembly RH and passenger side seat belt pretensioner.

steps.instruction: CHECK PASSENGER AIRBAG ON/OFF INDICATOR OPERATION (a) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (b) Check the passenger airbag ON/OFF indicator operation. HINT Refer to the normal condition of the passenger airbag ON/OFF indicator    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type)/Diagnosis System or See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type)/Diagnosis System). Result: B --  CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor assembly and the hazard warning signal switch assembly. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Check that the connectors (on the center airbag sensor assembly side and passenger seat belt warning light assembly side) are not damaged See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE WIRE HARNESS OK -- Continue to next step.

(b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connector from the center airbag sensor assembly. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (f) Check the passenger airbag ON/OFF indicator operation. OK: Neither ON nor OFF passenger airbag ON/OFF indicator comes on. OK --  CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY NG -- Continue to next step.

(c) Disconnect the connector from the passenger seat belt warning light assembly. (d) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 44 - Roll Over Cut Off Indicator Malfunction The curtain airbag cutoff switch is a mechanism that operates both the right and left side of the curtain shield airbag and the front seat outer belt when the center airbag sensor detects a rollover. The RSCA OFF indicator is installed in the combination meter. If the curtain airbag cutoff switch is operated, the indicator comes on to inform the driver that the rollover detection system is not working. The initial setting of the rollover detection system is on. It automatically operates every time the ignition switch is turned ON. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK RSCA OFF INDICATOR OPERATION (a) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (b) Check the RSCA OFF indicator operation. Result B --  CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS A -- Continue to next step.

(c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor assembly and the combination meter assembly. OK: The connectors are properly connected. dtcs.description: is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the passenger side buckle switch circuit. HINT * When DTC B1650/32 is detected as a result of troubleshooting the supplemental restraint system, check the DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU, and perform troubleshooting for those DTCs first. * Use the Techstream to check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTC cannot be read. HINT * If troubleshooting (wire harness inspection) is difficult to perform, remove the front passenger seat installation bolts to see the under surface of the seat cushion. * In the above case, hold the seat so that it does not tip over. Holding the seat up for a long period of time may cause problems, such as seat rail deformation. Hold the seat up only for as long as necessary. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Turn the ignition switch to the on position. (b) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type). NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the on position. dtcs.description: is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the seat position sensor circuit. HINT * When DTC B1650/32 is detected as a result of troubleshooting the supplemental restraint system, check the DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU, and perform troubleshooting for those DTCs first. * Use the Techstream to check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTC cannot be read. HINT * If troubleshooting (wire harness inspection) is difficult to perform, remove the passenger seat installation bolts to see the under surface of the seat cushion. * In the above case, hold the seat so that it does not tip over. Holding the seat up for a long period of time may cause problems, such as seat rail deformation. Hold the seat up only for as long as necessary. PROCEDURE

HINT First clear DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU and then in the center airbag sensor assembly. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (d) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (e) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (f) Replace the occupant classification ECU    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Occupant Classification Module - Air Bag/Service and Repair/Occupant Classification ECU (for Bench Seat Type)/Removal. (g) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (i) Load the data from the previous ECU stored in the Techstream, into the newly installed ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type)/Registration. (j) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (k) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (l) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type). HINT If DTC is not cleared at this time, past DTC will remain. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM SENSITIVITY CHECK (a) Perform the sensitivity check    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type)/Initialization. NEXT --  END

dtcs.description: Seat Belt Tension Sensor Power Source Circuit Malfunction The belt tension sensor circuit consists of the occupant classification ECU and the belt tension sensor. The belt tension sensor sends the belt tension signal to the occupant classification ECU to control the occupant classification system. DTCs B1773 and B1776 are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the belt tension sensor circuit. HINT * When DTC B1650/32 is detected as a result of troubleshooting the supplemental restraint system, check the DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU, and perform troubleshooting for those DTCs first. * Use the Techstream to check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTC cannot be read. HINT * If troubleshooting (wire harness inspection) is difficult to perform, remove the passenger seat installation bolts to see the under surface of the seat cushion. * In the above case, hold the seat so that it does not tip over. Holding the seat up for a long period of time may cause problems, such as seat rail deformation. Hold the seat up only for as long as necessary. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Pressure Sensor Circuit Malfunction

Pressure Sensor Power Source Circuit Malfunction The pressure sensor circuit consists of the occupant classification ECU and the occupant classification sensor. The occupant classification sensor sends the pressure signal to the occupant classification ECU to control the occupant classification system. DTCs B1774 and B1775 are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the pressure sensor circuit. HINT * When DTC B1650/32 is detected as a result of troubleshooting the supplemental restraint system, check the DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU, and perform troubleshooting for those DTCs first. * Use the Techstream to check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTC cannot be read. HINT * If troubleshooting (wire harness inspection) is difficult to perform, remove the passenger seat installation bolts to see the under surface of the seat cushion. * In the above case, hold the seat so that it does not tip over. Holding the seat up for a long period of time may cause problems, such as seat rail deformation. Hold the seat up only for as long as necessary. * After performing the zero point calibration, check for DTCs to confirm that the calibration is completed normally. (If it fails, DTC

is output when the following condition is met: * Zero point calibration is performed when the conditions for the calibration are not satisfied. * Zero point calibration has failed. Condition for the zero point calibration: HINT * When DTC B1650/32 is detected as a result of troubleshooting for the supplemental restraint system, check the DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU. When DTC B1797 is output, perform troubleshooting for the DTC. * Use the Techstream to check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTC cannot be read. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: is also set when the front seat with adjuster frame assembly RH is subjected to a strong impact, even if an actual accident has not occurred. However, when the occupant classification ECU outputs a collision detection signal, even if the vehicle is not in a collision, DTC B1785 can be cleared by conducting the zero point calibration and sensitivity check. Therefore, if DTC B1785 is set, first perform the zero point calibration and sensitivity check. HINT * When DTC B1650/32 is set as a result of troubleshooting the supplemental restraint system, perform troubleshooting for DTC

(c) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (d) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type). HINT First clear DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU and then in the center airbag sensor assembly. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (g) Check the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type). OK:

REPLACE OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION ECU (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (c) Store the occupant classification ECU data into the Techstream    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type)/Registration. HINT If the ECU data cannot be stored in the Techstream, replace the front seat cushion assembly (with occupant classification ECU and occupant classification sensor). (d) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (e) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (f) Replace the occupant classification ECU    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Occupant Classification Module - Air Bag/Service and Repair/Occupant Classification ECU (for Bench Seat Type)/Removal. (g) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (i) Load the data from the previous ECU stored in the Techstream, into the newly installed ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type)/Registration. (j) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (k) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (l) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type). HINT If DTC is not cleared at this time, past DTC will remain. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (b) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type). HINT First clear DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU and then in the center airbag sensor assembly. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (d) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (e) Check the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type). OK:

REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the center airbag sensor assembly    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM SENSITIVITY CHECK (a) Perform the sensitivity check    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Bench Seat Type)/Initialization. NEXT --  END

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY COMMUNICATION CIRCUIT (TO B+) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the No. 1 seat wire connector from the floor wire. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (f) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: NG --  CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (TO B+) OK --  REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY COMMUNICATION CIRCUIT (FOR OPEN) (a) Disconnect the No. 1 seat wire connector from the floor wire. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (FOR OPEN) OK --  REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY COMMUNICATION CIRCUIT (TO GROUND) (a) Disconnect the No. 1 seat wire connector from the floor wire. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (TO GROUND) OK --  REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (TO B+) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the floor wire connector from the instrument panel wire. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (f) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (FOR OPEN) (a) Disconnect the floor wire connector from the instrument panel wire. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (TO GROUND) (a) Disconnect the floor wire connector from the instrument panel wire. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE

dtcs.description: of the occupant classification system. * Use the Techstream to check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTCs cannot be read. HINT * If troubleshooting (wire harness inspection) is difficult to perform, remove the front RH seat assembly installation bolts to see the under surface of the seat cushion. * In the above case, hold the seat so that it does not fall down. Holding the seat for a long period of time may cause problems, such as seat rail deformation. Hold the seat up only for as long as necessary. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (b) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type). HINT * First clear DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU and then in the center airbag sensor assembly. * Use the Techstream to clear the DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTCs cannot be cleared. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (d) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (e) Using the Techstream, check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type). OK:

CHECK CONNECT CONNECTORS (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the occupant classification ECU and the occupant classification sensors. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Check that the connectors (on the occupant classification ECU side and the occupant classification sensors side) are not damaged    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE WIRE HARNESS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (TO B+) (a) Disconnect the connectors from the occupant classification ECU and the 4 occupant classification sensors. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (d) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (TO GROUND) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.
CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (FOR OPEN) (a) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.
CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (FOR SHORT) (a) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Connect the connectors to the occupant classification ECU and the 4 occupant classification sensors. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (d) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type). HINT * First clear DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU and then in the center airbag sensor assembly. * Use the Techstream to clear the DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTCs cannot be cleared. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (g) Using the Techstream, check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type). OK:

REPLACE OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION ECU (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the occupant classification ECU    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Occupant Classification Module - Air Bag/Service and Repair/Occupant Classification ECU (for Separate Seat Type)/Removal. HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle when possible. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (d) Using the Techstream, perform the zero point calibration    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type). OK: "Zero Point Calibration is complete." is displayed on the Techstream. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM SENSITIVITY CHECK (a) Using the Techstream, perform the sensitivity check    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type). Standard: 27 to 33 kg (59.52 to 72.75 lb) NEXT --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK

(i) Using the Techstream, check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type). OK:

REPLACE OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION ECU (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the occupant classification ECU    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Occupant Classification Module - Air Bag/Service and Repair/Occupant Classification ECU (for Separate Seat Type)/Removal. (d) Using the Techstream, perform the zero point calibration    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type). OK: "Zero Point Calibration is complete." is displayed on the Techstream. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM SENSITIVITY CHECK (a) Using the Techstream, perform the sensitivity check    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Occupant Classification System (for Separate Seat Type). Standard: 27 to 33 kg (59.52 to 72.75 lb) NEXT --  END Occupant Classification System (For Separate Seat Type)

dtcs.description: is set when a malfunction is detected in the occupant classification ECU. HINT * When DTC B1650/32 is detected as a result of troubleshooting the supplemental restraint system, check the DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU, and perform troubleshooting for those DTCs first. * Use the Techstream to check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTCs cannot be read. HINT * If troubleshooting (wire harness inspection) is difficult to perform, remove the front passenger seat installation bolts to see the under surface of the seat cushion. * In the above case, hold the seat so that it does not tip over. Holding the seat up for a long period of time may cause problems, such as seat rail deformation. Hold the seat up only for as long as necessary. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the spiral cable connectors (on the steering pad side) are not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, and the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Check that the spiral cable connectors (on the steering pad side) are properly connected. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SPIRAL CABLE (FOR OPEN) (a) Disconnect the spiral cable connectors from the instrument panel wire and steering pad. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (FOR OPEN) (a) Disconnect the instrument panel wire connector from the center airbag sensor assembly. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: OK --  CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the spiral cable connectors (on the steering pad side) are not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, and the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Check that the spiral cable connectors (on the steering pad side) are properly connected. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SPIRAL CABLE (TO GROUND) (a) Disconnect the spiral cable connectors from the instrument panel wire and steering pad. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (TO GROUND) (a) Disconnect the instrument panel wire connector from the center airbag sensor assembly. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: OK --  CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the spiral cable connectors (on the steering pad side) are not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, and the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Check that the spiral cable connectors (on the steering pad side) are properly connected. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SPIRAL CABLE (TO B+) (a) Disconnect the spiral cable connectors from the instrument panel wire and steering pad. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (d) Measure the voltage Standard voltage: NG --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (TO B+) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the instrument panel wire connector from the center airbag sensor assembly. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (f) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY HINT If continuing from step 18, begin from (a). If continuing from any other step, begin from (c). (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Connect the instrument panel wire connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (d) Using a service wire, connect D+ and D- of connector E. NOTICE: * Twist the end of the service wire in order to insert it into the connector. * Do not forcibly insert the twisted service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (i) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (j) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK: DTCs B1801, B1802 and B1803 are not output. HINT DTCs other than B1801, B1802 or B1803 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE STEERING PAD (DRIVE SIDE SQUIB) HINT If continuing from step 19, begin from (c). If continuing from any other step, being from (a). (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the service wire from connector E. (d) Connect the connectors to the steering pad. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (i) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (j) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK: DTCs B1800, B1801, B1802 and B1803 are not output. HINT DTCs other than B1800, B1801, B1802 or B1803 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG --  REPLACE STEERING PAD OK --  OTHERS USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the spiral cable connectors (on the steering pad side) are not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, or the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Check that the spiral cable connectors (on the steering pad side) are properly connected. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SPIRAL CABLE (a) Disconnect the spiral cable connectors from the instrument panel wire and steering pad. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (d) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector D    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (a) Restore the released activation prevention mechanism of connector B to the original condition. (b) Disconnect the instrument panel wire connector from the center airbag sensor assembly. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (e) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: (f) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (g) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (h) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: (i) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (j) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the steering pad    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Air Bag/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Pad/Removal. HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle when possible. (b) Connect the instrument panel wire to the airbag sensor assembly center and spiral cable. (c) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (f) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (g) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (i) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK: DTC 51 is not output. HINT DTCs other than 51 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY OK --  END

dtcs.description: 51 - Short to B+ in Driver Side Squib Circuit * The driver side squib circuit consists of the center airbag sensor assembly, the spiral cable and the steering pad. * The circuit signals the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. * These DTCs are set when a malfunction is detected in the driver side squib circuit.

(d) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

(d) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK:

steps.instruction: CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds.
