(b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (d) Using a service wire, connect A24-1 (P2+) and A24-2 (P2-) of connector E. NOTICE: * Twist the end of the service wire in order to insert it into the connector. * Do not forcibly insert the twisted service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. dtcs.description: 54 - Short to B+ in Front Passenger Side Squib 2nd Step Circuit The front passenger side squib (Dual stage -2nd step) circuit consists of the center airbag sensor assembly and the front passenger airbag assembly. The circuit signals the SRS to deploy when airbag deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are set when a malfunction is detected in the front passenger side squib (Dual stage - 2nd step) circuit.

steps.instruction: CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY HINT If continuing from step 6, begin from (a). If continuing from any other step, begin from (c). NOTICE: * Twist the end of the service wire in order to insert it into the connector. * Do not forcibly insert the twisted service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. dtcs.description: 57 - Short to B+ in Driver Side Curtain Shield Squib Circuit The curtain shield airbag (driver seat side) squib circuit consists of the center airbag sensor assembly and the curtain shield airbag assembly LH. The circuit signals the SRS to deploy when airbag deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are set when a malfunction is detected in the curtain shield airbag (driver seat side) squib circuit.

(d) Using a service wire, connect F17-1 (PD+) and F17-2 (PD-) of connector C. NOTICE: * Twist the end of the service wire in order to insert it into the connector. * Do not forcibly insert the twisted service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting. (d) Connect the connector to the front seat outer belt assembly LH. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (d) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance. CHECK FRONT SEAT OUTER BELT ASSEMBLY LH (PRETENSIONER SQUIB (DRIVER SEAT SIDE)) (a) Replace the front seat outer belt assembly LH    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Belt Systems/Seat Belt/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Front Seat Outer Belt Assembly (for Double Cab)/Removal,    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Belt Systems/Seat Belt/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Front Seat Outer Belt Assembly (for Access Cab)/Removal or See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Belt Systems/Seat Belt/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Front Seat Outer Belt Assembly (for Regular Cab)/Removal). dtcs.description: 73 - Short to B+ in Front Driver Side Pretensioner Squib Circuit The pretensioner squib (driver seat side) circuit consists of the center airbag sensor assembly and the front seat outer belt assembly LH. This circuit signals the SRS to deploy when airbag deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are set when a malfunction is detected in the pretensioner squib (driver seat side) circuit.

(h) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK: DTC 74 is not output. HINT DTCs other than 74 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY OK --  END

CHECK FRONT PASSENGER SIDE FRONT PRETENSIONER SQUIB CIRCUIT (a) Disconnect the floor wire connector from the center airbag sensor assembly. (b) Disconnect the No. 1 rear door wire connector from the front seat outer belt assembly RH. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (e) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: (f) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (g) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (h) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: (i) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (j) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: OK --  REPLACE FRONT SEAT OUTER BELT ASSEMBLY RH NG -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 1 REAR DOOR WIRE (a) Disconnect the floor wire connector from the No. 1 rear door wire. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position. (d) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector D    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 REAR DOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE FRONT SEAT OUTER BELT ASSEMBLY RH (a) Replace the front seat outer belt assembly RH    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Belt Systems/Seat Belt/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Front Seat Outer Belt Assembly (for Double Cab)/Removal,    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Belt Systems/Seat Belt/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Front Seat Outer Belt Assembly (for Access Cab)/Removal or See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Belt Systems/Seat Belt/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Front Seat Outer Belt Assembly (for Regular Cab)/Removal). HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle when possible. (b) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (e) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (g) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (h) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK: DTC 74 is not output. HINT DTCs other than 74 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY OK --  END

dtcs.description: 74 - Short to B+ in Front Passenger Side Front Pretensioner Squib Circuit The pretensioner squib (front passenger seat side) circuit consists of the center airbag sensor assembly and the front seat outer belt assembly RH. This circuit signals the SRS to deploy when airbag deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are set when a malfunction is detected in the pretensioner squib (front passenger seat side) circuit.

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Proceed to the appropriate step according to DTC readings. (1) If using the Techstream (read the 5-digit DTCs): Using the Techstream, check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. Result: (2) If not using the Techstream (read the 2-digit DTCs): Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. Result: E --  CHECK CONNECTOR D --  CHECK CONNECTOR C --  CHECK CONNECTOR B --  CHECK CONNECTOR A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connector (on the front seat outer belt assembly RH side) is not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, and the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 REAR DOOR WIRE (ACCESS CAB) NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE (EXCEPT ACCESS CAB) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor assembly and the front airbag sensor RH. Result: C --  CONNECT CONNECTORS B --  CHECK FRONT PASSENGER SIDE FRONT PRETENSIONER SQUIB CIRCUIT (FOR SHORT) A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT PASSENGER SIDE FRONT PRETENSIONER SQUIB CIRCUIT (FOR SHORT) (a) Disconnect the floor wire connectors from the center airbag sensor assembly and front seat outer belt assembly RH. (b) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (c) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY (a) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the LOCK position. (f) Turn the ignition switch to the ON position, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK:

dtcs.description: Push Switch / Key Unlock Warning Switch Malfunction This DTC will be output if the transponder key ECU does not detect that the unlock warning switch is ON even when the key is inserted into the ignition key cylinder. Under normal conditions, the unlock warning switch is ON when the key is inserted into the ignition key cylinder. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (UNLOCK WARNING SWITCH) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the ignition switch ON with the key that cannot start the engine. (c) On the Techstream, enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Immobiliser / Data List / Key SW. Read the values. Immobiliser OK: "ON" (key is in ignition key cylinder) appears on the screen. OK --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm System Transmitter/Service and Repair/Removal NG -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT UNLOCK WARNING SWITCH ASSEMBLY (a) Remove the unlock warning switch. (b) Measure the resistance of the switch. Standard resistance: (c) Reinstall the unlock warning switch. NG --  REPLACE UNLOCK WARNING SWITCH ASSEMBLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (TRANSPONDER KEY ECU - UNLOCK WARNING SWITCH) (a) Disconnect the T10 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the U1 switch connector. (c) Measure the resistance of the wire harness side connectors. Standard resistance: (d) Reconnect the unlock warning switch connector. (e) Reconnect the ECU connector. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm System Transmitter/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Antenna Coil Open / Short The transponder key coil receives key code signals from key's transponder chip. The coil is built into the transponder key amplifier, which amplifies the key code signals and output the signals to the transponder key ECU. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (TRANSPONDER KEY COIL) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the ignition switch ON with the key that cannot start the engine. (c) On the Techstream, enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Immobiliser / Data List / Antenna Coil Status. Read the values. Transponder key ECU assembly OK: "Normal" (Transponder key coil is normal) appears on the screen. OK --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm System Transmitter/Service and Repair/Removal NG -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY - TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER) (a) Disconnect the T10 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the T9 amplifier connector. (c) Measure the resistance of the wire harness side connectors. Standard resistance: (d) Reconnect the ECU connector. (e) Reconnect the amplifier connector. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm System Transponder/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Transponder Chip Malfunction This DTC is output when: 1) during key code registration, a key malfunction occurs; or 2) the key code was unable to be registered properly. Follow the below. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: RE-REGISTER KEY (a) Delete the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Re-register the key    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning, and check that the engine starts with the key. OK: Engine starts. NG --  REPLACE KEY OK --  NORMAL (INSPECTION FINISHED)

dtcs.description: Unmatched Encryption Code This DTC is output when a key with an incomplete key code is inserted into the ignition key cylinder. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: RE-REGISTER KEY (a) Delete the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) RE-register the key    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning, and check that the engine starts with the key. OK: Engine starts. NG --  REPLACE KEY OK --  END

dtcs.description: Unmatched Key Code This DTC is output when a key with a key code that has not been registered in the ECU is inserted into the ignition key cylinder. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: RE-REGISTER KEY (a) Delete the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Reregister the key, and check that the engine starts with the key    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. OK: Engine starts. NG --  REPLACE KEY OK --  NORMAL (INSPECTION FINISHED)

dtcs.description: No Communication in Immobiliser System

Communication Malfunction No. 2 When a key is inserted into the ignition key cylinder but no communication occurs between the key and transponder key ECU assembly, DTC B2796 is output. When a key is inserted into the ignition key cylinder but a communication error occurs between the key and transponder key ECU assembly,

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (ENGINE IMMOBILISER SYSTEM) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the ignition switch ON with the key that cannot start the engine. (c) On the Techstream enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Immobiliser / Data List / Immobiliser. Read the values. Immobiliser OK: "Unset" (ignition switch ON) appears on the screen. OK --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm System Transmitter/Service and Repair/Removal NG -- Continue to next step.

CHECK WHETHER ENGINE STARTS WITH OTHER KEYS (a) Check whether the engine starts with the vehicle's other keys. OK: Engine starts. OK --  RE- REGISTER OR REPLACE KEY THAT CANNOT START ENGINE NG -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the ignition switch ON with the key that will not start the engine. (c) On the Techstream, enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Immobiliser / Data List / Antenna Coil Status. Read the values. Immobiliser OK: "Normal" (transponder key coil is normal) appears on screen. OK --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER NG -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY - TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER) (a) Disconnect the T10 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the T9 amplifier connector. (c) Measure the resistance of the wire harness side connectors. Standard resistance: (d) Reconnect the ECU connector. (e) Reconnect the amplifier connector. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER (OPERATION) (a) After replacing the transponder key amplifier with a normally functioning amplifier    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm System Transponder/Service and Repair/Removal, check that the engine starts. OK: Engine starts. NG --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm System Transmitter/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  NORMAL (TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER DEFECTIVE)

dtcs.description: Communication Malfunction No. 1 This DTC is output when a communication error occurs between the transponder key amplifier and transponder key ECU. Some possible reasons for the communication error are: 1) 2 or more ignition keys are positioned too close together, or 2) noise is occurring in the communication line. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK KEYS (a) Check if the ignition key being used is near other ignition keys, as shown in the illustration. Also, check if the key ring is in contact with the key grip. Result B --  CHECK TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTCS (a) Separate the keys from each other and/or remove the key ring. (b) Delete the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Insert a key into the ignition cylinder. Remove it. Repeat for all the other keys. (d) Check that no code is output. OK: No code is output. OK --  NORMAL (INSPECTION FINISHED) NG -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY (a) Using an oscilloscope or the intelligent tester check for noise in the waveform between the terminals of the T9 amplifier connector and T10 ECU connector. OK: No noise is present (see illustration). NG --  FIND CAUSE OF NOISE AND REMOVE IT OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY - TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER) (a) Disconnect the T10 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the T9 amplifier connector. (c) Measure the resistance of the wire harness side connectors. Standard resistance: (d) Reconnect the ECU connector. (e) Reconnect the amplifier connector. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER (OPERATION) (a) After replacing the transponder key amplifier with a normally functioning amplifier    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm System Transponder/Service and Repair/Removal, check that the engine starts. OK: Engine starts. NG --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm System Transmitter/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  END (TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER DEFECTIVE)

dtcs.description: Engine Immobiliser System Malfunction This DTC is output when one of the following occurs: 1) the ECM detects errors in its own communications with the immobiliser code ECU; 2) the ECM detects errors in the communication lines; or 3) the ECU communication ID between the immobiliser code ECU and ECM is different and an engine start is attempted. Before troubleshooting for this DTC, make sure no certification ECU DTCs are present. If present, troubleshoot the certification ECU DTCs first. HINT * If the transponder key ECU assembly is replaced, register the key and ECU communication ID. * If the ECM is replaced, register the ECU communication ID. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTC. OK:
ECU COMMUNICATION ID REGISTRATION (a) Re-register the ECU communication ID between the transponder key ECU assembly and ECM. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTC. OK:

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the ECM and the transponder key ECU. OK: Connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECTING CONNECTORS PROPERLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY - ECM) (a) Disconnect the T10 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the E7 or E14 ECM connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: *1: for 1GR-FE *2: for 2TR-FE (d) Reconnect the ECU connector. (e) Reconnect the amplifier connector. MG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE ECM (a) Replace the ECM. for 1GR-FE    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. for 2TR-FE    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
ECU COMMUNICATION ID REGISTRATION (a) Re-register the ECU communication ID between the transponder key ECU assembly and ECM. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTC. OK:
